items.teach_script: The word for 'I' in Indonesian is 'saya'. Saya.

teach_segments.text: The word for 'I' in Indonesian is

items.teach_script: 'To want' is 'mau'. Mau. So 'I want' is — saya mau.
'To eat' is 'makan'. Makan.
'To drink' is 'minum'. Minum.

prompts.cue_en: How would you say: I want?
How would you say: I want to eat?
And: I want to drink?
Just the word: to eat.
Just the word: to drink.
Again — I want to eat.
And again — I want to drink.
Just: I.
Just: want.
One more time, the whole thing: I want to eat.
prompts.decompose_script: Piece by piece: saya — mau — makan.

prompts.cue_en: Build it: I want to drink.
prompts.decompose_script: Piece by piece: saya — mau — minum.

prompts.cue_en: Last one for today: I want to eat.
prompts.decompose_script: Saya mau — I want — then makan.

items.teach_script: 'You' is 'kamu'. Kamu.
'Not' is 'tidak'. Tidak. It sits right before the word it cancels: saya tidak mau — I don't want.

teach_segments.text: It sits right before the word it cancels:

items.teach_script: Indonesian questions are free. Say the sentence and let your voice rise at the end: kamu mau makan? — do you want to eat? Nothing else changes.

teach_segments.text: Indonesian questions are free. Say the sentence and let your voice rise at the end:
— do you want to eat? Nothing else changes.

items.teach_script: In everyday speech, when it's obvious who you mean, Indonesians drop the pronoun. 'Mau makan?' — want to eat? Both ways are right; you'll hear the short one constantly.

teach_segments.text: In everyday speech, when it's obvious who you mean, Indonesians drop the pronoun.
— want to eat? Both ways are right; you'll hear the short one constantly.

prompts.cue_en: A statement: you want to eat.
Now make it a question — do you want to eat?
How would you say: I don't want?
I don't want to eat.
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak sits right before mau: saya — tidak mau — makan.

prompts.cue_en: I don't want to drink.
prompts.decompose_script: Same shape: saya — tidak mau — minum.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: do you want to drink?
Tell me: you don't want to eat.
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu, then tidak mau, then makan.

prompts.cue_en: Two words, like to a friend: want to eat?
Ask me: don't you want to drink?
prompts.decompose_script: It's the statement with rising voice: kamu tidak mau minum?

prompts.cue_en: Just: you.
Quick: not.
You don't want to drink.
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu — tidak mau — minum.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: do you want to eat?
And: I don't want to eat.
prompts.decompose_script: Saya — tidak mau — makan.

items.teach_script: 'To go' is 'pergi'. Pergi.
'To' — toward a place — is 'ke'. Ke.
'The market' is 'pasar'. Pasar. So 'to the market' — ke pasar.
'Now' is 'sekarang'. Sekarang. Time words like this usually sit at the end of the sentence.

teach_segments.text: Time words like this usually sit at the end of the sentence.

prompts.cue_en: How would you say: I want to go?
To the market — I want to go to the market.
prompts.decompose_script: The journey is one chunk: pergi ke pasar — go to the market.

prompts.cue_en: Just: now.
I want to eat now.
prompts.decompose_script: Sekarang — now — goes at the end: saya mau makan, then sekarang.

prompts.cue_en: I want to go now.
prompts.decompose_script: Saya mau pergi — then sekarang at the end.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: do you want to go to the market?
prompts.decompose_script: Statement plus rising voice: kamu mau — pergi ke pasar?

prompts.cue_en: I don't want to go now.
prompts.decompose_script: Two chunks: tidak mau — pergi sekarang.

prompts.cue_en: Just: the market.
Ask me: do you want to drink now?
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu mau minum — then sekarang at the end, rising.

prompts.cue_en: I don't want to go to the market.
expected: saya tidak mau pergi ke pasar
aku tidak mau pergi ke pasar
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak mau, then the journey: pergi ke pasar.

prompts.cue_en: Again: I want to go.
Now the whole thing: I want to go to the market now.
expected: saya mau pergi ke pasar sekarang
aku mau pergi ke pasar sekarang
prompts.decompose_script: Three pieces: saya mau — pergi ke pasar — sekarang.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: do you want to go to the market now?
expected: kamu mau pergi ke pasar sekarang
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu mau — pergi ke pasar — sekarang — and let it rise.

prompts.cue_en: And: I don't want to go to the market now.
expected: saya tidak mau pergi ke pasar sekarang
aku tidak mau pergi ke pasar sekarang
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak mau, then pergi ke pasar, then sekarang at the end.

items.teach_script: 'Can' is 'bisa'. Bisa. It works exactly like mau: saya bisa — I can.
'Have to' — must — is 'harus'. Harus.
'Later' is 'nanti'. Nanti. Another time word — it goes at the end, like sekarang.

teach_segments.text: Another time word — it goes at the end, like

items.teach_script: 'To go home' has its own word: 'pulang'. Pulang. Not 'go to house' — just pulang.

teach_segments.text: 'To go home' has its own word:

prompts.cue_en: How would you say: I can?
I can go now.
prompts.decompose_script: Bisa where mau would sit: saya bisa — pergi — sekarang.

prompts.cue_en: I have to eat.
Just: to go home.
I want to go home.
I have to go home now.
prompts.decompose_script: Harus — have to — then pulang, then sekarang at the end.

prompts.cue_en: Just: later.
I can eat later.
prompts.decompose_script: Bisa sits where mau would: saya bisa — makan — nanti.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: can you go to the market later?
expected: kamu bisa pergi ke pasar nanti
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu bisa — pergi ke pasar — nanti — rising at the end.

prompts.cue_en: I can't go now.
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak bisa — can't: saya tidak bisa — pergi sekarang.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: do you have to go home?
The big one: I can't go to the market now.
expected: saya tidak bisa pergi ke pasar sekarang
aku tidak bisa pergi ke pasar sekarang
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak bisa, then the journey — pergi ke pasar — then sekarang.

prompts.cue_en: Tell me: you have to go home now.
And: I don't want to go to the market later.
expected: saya tidak mau pergi ke pasar nanti
aku tidak mau pergi ke pasar nanti
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak mau — pergi ke pasar — nanti.

items.teach_script: 'This' is 'ini'. Ini.
'That' is 'itu'. Itu.
'Good' — for things — is 'bagus'. Bagus. And there's no word for 'is': ini bagus — this is good. Just point and say it.

teach_segments.text: — this is good. Just point and say it.

items.teach_script: 'But' is 'tapi'. Tapi. It glues two little sentences together.

teach_segments.text: It glues two little sentences together.

prompts.cue_en: How would you say: this is good?
prompts.decompose_script: No word for 'is' — just: ini bagus.

prompts.cue_en: That is good.
This is not good.
I want this.
I want that.
Ask me: do you want this?
I don't want that.
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak mau, then point at it: itu.

prompts.cue_en: Just: but.
That is good, but I want this.
expected: itu bagus tapi saya mau ini
itu bagus tapi aku mau ini
prompts.decompose_script: Two small sentences glued with tapi: itu bagus — tapi — saya mau ini.

prompts.cue_en: This is good, but that is not good.
expected: ini bagus tapi itu tidak bagus
prompts.decompose_script: Ini bagus — tapi — itu tidak bagus.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: is that good?
That is good, but I don't want that.
expected: itu bagus tapi saya tidak mau itu
itu bagus tapi aku tidak mau itu
prompts.decompose_script: Itu bagus — tapi — saya tidak mau itu.

prompts.cue_en: I want to go home, but I have to eat now.
expected: saya mau pulang tapi saya harus makan sekarang
aku mau pulang tapi aku harus makan sekarang
prompts.decompose_script: First clause, tapi, second clause — each one you already know.

prompts.cue_en: I want to go, but I can't go now.
expected: saya mau pergi tapi saya tidak bisa pergi sekarang
aku mau pergi tapi aku tidak bisa pergi sekarang
prompts.decompose_script: Saya mau pergi — tapi — saya tidak bisa pergi sekarang.

items.teach_script: Here is the whole Indonesian past tense: 'sudah' — already. Sudah. Saya sudah makan — I already ate, I have eaten. The verb never changes.

teach_segments.text: Here is the whole Indonesian past tense:
— I already ate, I have eaten. The verb never changes.

items.teach_script: 'Not yet' is 'belum'. Belum. Saya belum makan — I haven't eaten yet. It sits exactly where sudah would.

teach_segments.text: — I haven't eaten yet. It sits exactly where

items.teach_script: 'To buy' is 'beli'. Beli.
When someone asks a sudah question — kamu sudah makan? — you answer with the time word itself: 'Sudah' — already — or 'Belum' — not yet. Not 'yes' or 'no'.

teach_segments.text: — you answer with the time word itself:

prompts.cue_en: The past, in one word extra: I already ate — I've eaten.
I haven't eaten yet.
Ask me: have you eaten?
prompts.decompose_script: Literally: you already eat? — kamu sudah makan?

prompts.cue_en: Answer me: yes, I have — already.
Answer me: not yet.
I want to buy this.
prompts.decompose_script: Mau beli — want to buy — then ini.

prompts.cue_en: I already bought that.
prompts.decompose_script: Sudah before the verb: saya sudah — beli itu.

prompts.cue_en: I haven't bought that yet.
prompts.decompose_script: Belum sits where sudah would: saya belum — beli itu.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: did you already go to the market?
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu sudah — pergi ke pasar — rising at the end.

prompts.cue_en: I already went to the market.
prompts.decompose_script: Saya sudah — then the journey: pergi ke pasar.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: haven't you bought this yet?
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu belum — beli ini — and let it rise.

prompts.cue_en: Big sentence: I already went to the market, but I haven't bought that yet.
expected: saya sudah pergi ke pasar tapi saya belum beli itu
aku sudah pergi ke pasar tapi aku belum beli itu
saya sudah ke pasar tapi belum beli itu
prompts.decompose_script: Sudah clause — tapi — belum clause.

prompts.cue_en: I want to buy this, but this is not good.
expected: saya mau beli ini tapi ini tidak bagus
aku mau beli ini tapi ini tidak bagus
prompts.decompose_script: Saya mau beli ini — tapi — ini tidak bagus.

prompts.cue_en: I haven't eaten yet, but I can eat later.
expected: saya belum makan tapi saya bisa makan nanti
aku belum makan tapi aku bisa makan nanti
prompts.decompose_script: Belum makan — tapi — bisa makan nanti.

items.teach_script: And here is the whole future tense: 'akan' — will. Akan. Saya akan pergi — I will go. Again, the verb never changes.

teach_segments.text: And here is the whole future tense:
— I will go. Again, the verb never changes.

items.teach_script: 'Tomorrow' is 'besok'. Besok. A time word — so it goes at the end.

teach_segments.text: A time word — so it goes at the end.

items.teach_script: 'Because' is 'karena'. Karena. Like tapi, it glues two sentences — and now you can give reasons.

teach_segments.text: it glues two sentences — and now you can give reasons.

prompts.cue_en: The future: I will go.
Just: tomorrow.
I will go tomorrow.
prompts.decompose_script: Akan before the verb, besok at the end.

prompts.cue_en: I will go home tomorrow.
prompts.decompose_script: Akan pulang — will go home — then besok at the end.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: will you go to the market tomorrow?
expected: kamu akan pergi ke pasar besok
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu akan — pergi ke pasar — besok — and let it rise.

prompts.cue_en: I will buy that tomorrow.
prompts.decompose_script: Akan beli itu — then besok at the end.

prompts.cue_en: Just: because.
I want to go home because I haven't eaten yet.
expected: saya mau pulang karena saya belum makan
aku mau pulang karena aku belum makan
prompts.decompose_script: The reason comes after karena: saya mau pulang — karena — saya belum makan.

prompts.cue_en: I can't go now because I have to eat.
expected: saya tidak bisa pergi sekarang karena saya harus makan
aku tidak bisa pergi sekarang karena aku harus makan
prompts.decompose_script: First: tidak bisa pergi sekarang. Then karena. Then: saya harus makan.

prompts.cue_en: I will eat later because I want to go now.
expected: saya akan makan nanti karena saya mau pergi sekarang
aku akan makan nanti karena aku mau pergi sekarang
prompts.decompose_script: Akan makan nanti — karena — mau pergi sekarang.

prompts.cue_en: Ask me: will you buy this?
prompts.decompose_script: Kamu akan — beli ini — rising at the end.

prompts.cue_en: I won't go tomorrow.
prompts.decompose_script: Tidak before akan: tidak akan — won't.

prompts.cue_en: The big one: I can't go to the market now because I have to eat.
expected: saya tidak bisa pergi ke pasar sekarang karena saya harus makan
aku tidak bisa pergi ke pasar sekarang karena aku harus makan
prompts.decompose_script: Three pieces: tidak bisa pergi ke pasar sekarang — karena — saya harus makan.

prompts.cue_en: I will go to the market tomorrow because I haven't bought that yet.
expected: saya akan pergi ke pasar besok karena saya belum beli itu
aku akan pergi ke pasar besok karena aku belum beli itu
prompts.decompose_script: Akan pergi ke pasar besok — karena — belum beli itu.

prompts.cue_en: Last one this week: that is good, but I will buy this tomorrow.
expected: itu bagus tapi saya akan beli ini besok
itu bagus tapi aku akan beli ini besok
prompts.decompose_script: Itu bagus — tapi — saya akan beli ini besok.

victory_intro.text: Last stretch — you build these yourself.
